narrative:
The Zurich Zebras are positioned for a high-ceiling offensive output today, anchored by the elite 112.19 projection for Pete Crow-Armstrong and Yordan Alvarez’s massive 106.06 rating. Crow-Armstrong benefits from a significant platoon advantage and a favorable matchup against Jake Irvin, while Alvarez exploits a 30% SP skill edge and optimal lineup positioning. Trea Turner and Junior Caminero round out the high-leverage core, both providing substantial value through favorable lineup slots and calculated platoon boosts that maximize our Roto efficiency across the board.

Caution is warranted with our platoon-sensitive bats, specifically Kyle Schwarber and Christian Yelich, who face difficult left-on-left matchups today. Their efficiency scores remain viable, but their production could be stifled by the projected pitcher matchups. Logan O'Hoppe and Carson Benge enter the day as our most volatile plays, weighed down by bottom-third batting order assignments. However, keep an eye on Turner’s BvP track record—his +15% historical edge against MacKenzie Gore suggests he remains a must-start despite the pitcher’s strikeout profile, provided the game-time conditions don't deteriorate.
